title: Fixing Invalid Node Traversal in a Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Given the root of a binary tree, return the values of all nodes at each level from left to right in a vector of vectors.
statement.inputFormat: A binary tree represented as a vector of pairs <a, b>, where <a, b> represents a node with left child <a> and right child <b>.
statement.outputFormat: A vector of vectors, where each sub-vector represents the values of all nodes at a given level from left to right.
statement.notes: There is a bug in the given implementation.

examples.explanation: The first vector represents the values of all nodes at each level from left to right.

hints.text: Check the code for DFS traversal.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: To solve this problem, we can use a level order traversal of the binary tree.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use DFS traversal and process all nodes at a given level before moving on to the next level.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a queue data structure to implement a level order traversal.
commonMistakes: Not using a queue for level order traversal
Processing nodes at a given level before moving on to the next level

solutions.python:
import sys
from typing import List

class TreeNode:
    def __init__(self, x):
        self.val = x
        self.left = None
        self.right = None

class Solution:
    def levelOrder(self, root: TreeNode) -> List[List[int]]:
        if not root:
            return []
        res = []
        queue = [root]
        while queue:
            level = []
            for _ in range(len(queue)):
                node = queue.pop(0)
                level.append(node.val)
                if node.left:
                    queue.append(node.left)
                if node.right:
                    queue.append(node.right)
            res.append(level)
        return res

if __name__ == '__main__':
    root = TreeNode(1)
    root.left = TreeNode(2)
    root.right = TreeNode(3)
    root.left.left = TreeNode(4)
    root.left.right = TreeNode(5)
    root.right.right = TreeNode(6)
    root.right.right.right = TreeNode(7)
    sol = Solution()
    print(sol.levelOrder(root))
